LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF COMPWEST INSURANCE COMPANY'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE AMENDMENT TO COMPLAINT IS GRANTED PURSUANT TO CCP 473(A) IN THE FURTHERANCE OF JUSTICE. THE TRIAL DATE WILL BE CONTINUED UNTIL FEBRUARY 10, 2014 TO ALLOW DEFENDANT CONTRERAS TO FILE A MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/ADJUDICATION. THE MOTION IS DENIED AS TO CCP 474; MOVING PARTY DID NOT SHOW THAT IT WAS GENUINELY IGNORANT OF THE POTENTIAL LIABILITY AND IDENTITY OF TROPICANA & RUSSELL INC., DBA MARINA FOOD AFTER MOVING PARTY'S AGENT (TRANSPAC SOLUTIONS) EXCHANGED CORRESPONDENCE WITH ARGONAUT GREAT CENTRAL INSURANCE COMPANY ON OCTOBER 26, 2009 AND NOVEMBER 10, 2009 (EXHIBIT A TO DECLARATION OF SEAN MORIARTY FILED IN OPPOSITION TO MOTION.) (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN #6185.

LAW AND MOTION 302, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT MANUEL PEDROSA CONTRERAS' MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT AGAINST PLAINTIFF-IN-INTERVENTION CANIZLES' IS DENIED. MOVING PARTY MR. CONTRERAS HAS NOT SATISFIED HIS BURDEN OF PRODUCTION UNDER CCP 437C THAT THE "RELEASE" APPLIES TO CLAIMS AGAINST CONTRERAS. THE RELEASE EXTENDS TO "TROPICANA AND RUSSELL INC., DBA MARINA FOODS, THEIR HEIRS, SUCCESSORS, INSURERS AND ASSIGNS." MOVING PARTY HAS NOT ESTABLISHED THAT THIS INCLUDES MR. CONTRERAS. EVEN IF THE BURDEN SHIFTED TO PLAINTIFF-IN-INTERVENTION MR. CANIZALES, THERE IS A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT WHETHER MR. CANIZALES KNEW AT THE TIME OF THE ACCIDENT, AND AT THE TIME MR. CANIZALES SIGNED THE RELEASE, THAT CONTRERAS WAS AN EMPLOYEE OF TROPICANA AND RUSSELL INC., DBA MARINA FOODS. (SEE EVIDENCE CITED IN OPPOSITION TO CONTRERAS' UNDISPUTED MATERIAL FACT 4.) ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185 =(302/MJM)

Motion For Leave To File Amendment To Complaint;
Off Calendar May-01-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: MINH N. HOANG, ESQ., 800.425.0967, FOR PLAINTIFF COMPWEST INSURANCE COMPANY.
STEVEN L. COSTA, ESQ., 415.986.1338 X143, FOR INTERVENOR DAVID CANIZALES.
SEAN P. MORIARTY, ESQ., 415.391.1113 X15, FOR DEFENDANT MANUEL PEDROSA CONTRERAS.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling WITH MODIFICATION: PLAINTIFF COMPWEST INSURANCE COMPANY's Motion For Leave To File Amendment To Complaint is granted pursuant to CCP 473(a) in the furtherance of justice. The trial date will be continued until FEBRUARY 10, 2014 to allow Defendant Contreras to file a motion for summary judgment/adjudication. The motion is denied as to CCP 474; moving party did not show that it was genuinely ignorant of the potential liability and identity of Tropicana & Russell Inc., dba Marina Food after moving party's agent (TransPac Solutions) exchanged correspondence with Argonaut Great Central Insurance Company on October 26, 2009 and November 10, 2009 (Exhibit A to Declaration of Sean Moriarty filed in opposition to motion.) (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Anthony Vaughn #6185.

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Apr-26-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. MINH H. HOANG OF GIBSON & SHARPS, PSC, TEL. NO. 1-800-425-0967 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE;
ATTY. SEAN MORIARTY OF CESARI, WERNER & MORIARTY APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT MANUEL CONTRERAS;
ATTY. STEVEN L. COSTA, TEL. NO. 986-1338 X 143 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT CANIZALES.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT MANUEL PEDROSA CONTRERAS' MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT AGAINST PLAINTIFF-IN-INTERVENTION CANIZLES' is denied. Moving party Mr. Contreras has not satisfied his burden of production under CCP 437c that the "Release" applies to claims against Contreras. The Release extends to "Tropicana and Russell Inc., dba Marina Foods, their heirs, successors, insurers and assigns." Moving party has not established that this includes Mr. Contreras. Even if the burden shifted to plaintiff-in-intervention Mr. Canizales, there is a triable issue of fact whether Mr. Canizales knew at the time of the accident, and at the time Mr. Canizales signed the Release, that Contreras was an employee of Tropicana and Russell Inc., dba Marina Foods. (See evidence cited in opposition to Contreras' undisputed material fact 4.) ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =(302/MJM)
